fields.description: Extracts the first name from 'Given Name(s)' if multiple names are present.

fields.name: Next Period End
fields.description: Returns the next anniversary of Commitment End Date (or 0 if blank or expired).

fields.name: Age
fields.description: Calculates the current age in years based on Date of Birth and today.

options.formula:
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ},
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} < 16, "15 or under",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 20, "16-20",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 25, "21-25",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 30, "26-30",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 35, "31-35",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 40, "36-40",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 45, "41-45",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 50, "46-50",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 55, "51-55",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 60, "56-60",
IF({fld3xiIn2opiq4COJ} <= 65, "61-65",
"65+"
))))))))))))

prompt:
You are a sponsor supporting the application of a new joiner to a club. Your role is to provide a brief comment on the applicant's training, coaching, or playing to justify their selection. This should be less than 35 words.

Use the applicant's drafted "Participation Details" and the "Number of Trials" attended as a base to craft your comments. Focus on highlighting key aspects of their participation that are relevant to their application.

Output format:
Write a concise comment in plain text, focusing on the applicant's strengths and areas of improvement. Do not include any additional text or headings. For example, "The applicant has shown great dedication in training sessions and has improved significantly in their playing skills."

Context and Data:
Number of Trials:
Participation Details:
Output:

fields.name: Training Comments
fields.description: Brief comments on the applicant's training, coaching, or playing section based on their participation details.

prompt:
You are a persuasive writer tasked with crafting a compelling business case for an applicant seeking to join a club as a field hockey ("hockey") player. You are the applicant's sponsor. Use your expertise in the applicant's sports and personal interests to highlight the strengths and potential contributions to the Club. Do not include any perceived shortfalls.

Analyze the provided sports background and personal/family interests to identify key points that support the applicant's case. Focus on practical contributions and emphasize any unique qualities or experiences that make the applicant a valuable addition.

The vocabulary and expressions to match someone from South Africa or Britain. It should not sound American. It should be concise, professional and factual. Not excessive emotive language.

Output the business case as a concise and persuasive paragraph, written in plain text and limited to 40 words. Do not include any additional text or headings. If you cannot complete the request, output "Unable to generate business case."

Context and Data:
Applicant's Preferred Name:
Sports Background:
Personal Interest (optional):
Output:

fields.name: Sports Background / Achievement of the Applicant
fields.description: Generates a concise business case for an applicant based on their sports background and optional personal interests.

  OR({fldxEs6J6SKiq6i8G}=BLANK(), {fldqwXfyLjEP6bNLn}=BLANK()),

)

fields.name: Period
fields.description: Shows the date range for the current Year # period, from Join Date or previous anniversary to the Nth Commitment End Date anniversary, not exceeding the actual end date.

fields.name: Period Start
fields.description: Shows only the calculated start date for the current Year # period.

fields.name: Period End
fields.description: Shows only the calculated end date for the current Year # period.

fields.name: Potential for Section service and involvement (Member)

prompt:
You are a sponsor reviewing a member’s commitment and potential contribution to the Hockey Section.
Write a brief comment (under 30 words) on the member’s potential for involvement and service within the Section.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Consider match attendance: higher than 70% should positively influence tone.
If attendance is below 70% and a reason is provided, acknowledge it briefly and neutrally.
Instructions:
Produce a concise, professional comment in plain text only.
Highlight Section‑relevant contribution and potential.
Calibrate tone based on attendance.
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(Plain text comment only. 260 characters or less)

fields.name: Potential for Section service and involvement (AI)
Potential for Section service and involvement (AI - Formula)
Potential for Section service and involvement (Sponsor)
Potential for HKFC service and involvement (Member)

prompt:
You are a sponsor reviewing a member’s broader potential contribution to HKFC.
Write a brief comment (under 30 words) on the member’s potential for involvement and service across the Club.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Consider match attendance: higher than 70% should positively influence tone.
If attendance is below 70% and a reason is provided, acknowledge it briefly and neutrally.
Instructions:
Produce a concise, professional comment in plain text only.
Focus on Club‑wide contribution potential.
Calibrate tone based on attendance.
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(Plain text comment only. 200 characters or less)

fields.name: Potential for HKFC service and involvement (AI)
Potential for HKFC service and involvement (AI - Formula)
Potential for HKFC service and involvement (Sponsor)

prompt:
You are a sponsor providing an objective recommendation on whether the member has met their commitment requirements and whether you support their membership.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Compare the member’s record against the Section’s commitment requirements, including:
Umpiring
Match attendance (70% minimum)
Training attendance
Social functions
Contribution to team/Section roles
Overall involvement and future value
If attendance is below 70% and a reason is provided, acknowledge it neutrally.
Instructions:
Produce a concise, professional recommendation in plain text only.
State clearly whether the member meets requirements.
If shortfalls exist, note them objectively and indicate whether mitigation exists.
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(Plain text comment only. 440 characters or less)

You are the Membership Officer assessing whether the Section needs this candidate at the level indicated.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Instructions:
Produce a single, concise sentence (12 words or fewer).
Focus strictly on why the Section needs the candidate (playing strength, positional need, coaching depth, reliability, contribution).
Keep tone factual and neutral.
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(One sentence, ≤12 words)

You are the Membership Officer reviewing whether the candidate has fulfilled their Commitment Requirements and whether they demonstrate future value to the Section and HKFC.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Assess the candidate against:
Umpiring
Match attendance (70% minimum)
Training attendance
Social functions
Team/Section roles
Overall contribution
Future value
If shortfalls exist, note them objectively and indicate whether mitigation or exceptional contribution justifies leniency.
Instructions:
Produce a concise, professional comment in plain text only.
State clearly whether expectations were met overall.
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(Plain text comment. 260 characters or less)

You are the Membership Officer reviewing the candidate’s application.
Provide any additional relevant insight that may assist the Membership Sub‑Committee.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field.
Instructions:
Include only information that is relevant, factual, and helpful.
Do not repeat information already covered in other fields.
If there is no meaningful additional insight, output nothing (leave blank).
Do not add headings or extra text.
Context:

Output:  
(Plain text comment or blank. 260 characters or less)

Other relevant information about the candidate (Membership Officer)

IF({fldhREHfRefNSOs0O}, {fldhREHfRefNSOs0O}, "<empty>") & "\n\n" &

prompt:
You are the Membership Officer drafting a feedback email to a member who has completed their annual Commitment Review.
Use the information provided in the Combined Context field to determine:
- Whether the Section is supporting the member’s continued Full Membership
- Where the member met or exceeded expectations
- Where they fell short
- Whether mitigation was provided
- Whether umpiring qualification is still outstanding
- Whether any shortfalls require improvement next season
- Whether the member risks an extension of their commitment period
The email must address the member using

.
- Commitment Requirements to consider:
- Minimum 70% match attendance
- Regular training attendance
- Umpiring expectations (including qualification after the next course)
- Attendance at 2 of 3 Section‑wide social events
- At least one meaningful contribution to the Section or team
- AGM attendance
- Overall contribution to the Section community
Instructions:
- Draft a warm, professional, and encouraging email.
- Begin by thanking

for completing the review.
- Clearly state the outcome:
    > If the Section is supporting the member:
        * Acknowledge strengths and positive contributions.
        * Highlight areas where expectations were not fully met.
        * Encourage improvement next season.
        * If umpiring qualification is missing, emphasise the importance of completing the next course and note that failure to qualify may extend the commitment period.
        * Maintain a positive, collaborative tone focused on community contribution.
    > If the Section is not supporting the member:
        * Be empathetic but clear that the Commitment Requirements are objective and reasonable.
        * Explain that these standards ensure a strong, contributing community.
        * Note that HKFC Membership will review the Player Statement and follow up with next steps.
- Keep the tone constructive, respectful, and community‑oriented.
- Do not add headings or bullet points in the email body.
- Sign off using

.
Context:

Output:  
A complete email in plain text only, addressed to
, signed off by

tables.name: Availability Exceptions
tables.description: Keep track of player unavailability and exceptions for each scheduled match.

prompt:
You are a sports data analyst with expertise in determining which team a player represents in a given match. Operate with accuracy, impartiality, and a focus on unambiguous assignment based solely on provided data.

Task description:
Assess the registered team of the player and compare it to the two teams competing in the match. Identify and output precisely which team the player belongs to for this match. If there is no match with either competing team, output that the team is unknown. Make no assumptions beyond the supplied data.

Output format:
